observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:

Reconcile the cashbook balances with the subsidiary ledgers to ensure accuracy of the cash accounts;

Prepare and timely submit bank reconciliation statements for all accounts maintained by the Municipality and ensure that balances reflected in the BRS are reconciled with the balances in the subsidiary ledger; and

Prepare and immediately submit a reconciliation of the surplus/ deficit from ordinary activities with the net cash flow from operating activities.
observations.reason: This was not acted by management. No reconciliation was made during the year and there were four (4) bank accounts not prepared with BRS. Also, no reconciliation was also done on the net cash flows from operating activities to surplus/ deficit. Reiterated as Finding No. 1 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the above observations, we recommended the Municipal Treasurer as Property Officer-designate to perform periodic reconciliation of property records maintained to ensure the veracity of the reported amounts of PPE in the financial statements.

Also, please conduct physical count on the twenty-three PPE accounts.

The following provisions of Sec. 490 of Government Accounting and Auditing Manual shall also be emphasized:

“Inventories of supplies, materials and equipment. — Physical stock-taking is an indispensable procedure for checking the integrity of property custodianship. In all cases, the physical inventory-taking which is required semi-annually or annually should be regarded with importance.

For effective control of government property, the COA prescribes the following regulations:

Chiefs of agencies are required to take a physical inventory of all the equipment and supplies belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, unless otherwise determined by the COA Chairman in specific cases. Such inventory shall be made as of December 31 on General Form 41 (A) (Appendix 12), and submitted to the Auditor not later than January 31 of each year, unless extended by the Chairman, Commission on Audit, upon prior request of the chief of agency concerned. When the exigencies of the service permit, the taking of inventory should be in the charge of a committee of two or more employees designated by the chief of agency, including the property officer or custodian, depending on the extent of property accountability in a particular agency, to be witnessed by a representative of the Agency Auditor.

Xxx”

The required annual/semi-annual inventory shall be certified correct by the committee in charge thereof, attested by the representative of the auditor concerned and approved by the chief of agency in interest.”
observations.reason: This was not prioritized by the management. Physical count of PPE was not conducted during the year. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office only submitted the list of property and equipment owned by the LGU. Variance of P65,975,061.43 was noted between the records of the Municipal Treasurers Office and Accounting Office. Reiterated as Finding No. 2 in this year’s Annual Audit Report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

     1. Immediately reconcile the cashbook  balances  with  the  subsidiary
        ledgers to ensure accuracy of the cash accounts;


     2. Prepare and timely submit bank reconciliation  statements  for  all
        accounts maintained by the Municipality and  ensure  that  balances
        reflected in the BRS  are  reconciled  with  the  balances  in  the
        subsidiary ledger; and


     3.  Prepare  and  immediately   submit   a   reconciliation   of   the
        surplus/deficit from ordinary activities with  the  net  cash  flow
        from operating activities.

We recommended the Management the following recommendations:
     1. That the MTO and the MAO must establish an effective and  practical
        system that would ensure the swift and regular reconciliation of
        records of both offices pertaining to the Municipality’s  Property,
        Plant and Equipment;


     2. Conduct a complete physical count of all properties;

     3. Implement a system of property management such  as  using  property
        tags and assigning property numbers  for  each  of  the  properties
        owned to facilitate easy identification; and

     4. That the MTO correctly use  the  appropriate  form,  which  is  the
        RPCPPE, and provide all the  necessary  data  required  for  proper
        identification of all PPE items.

We recommended that the Municipal Accounting  Office  prepare  and  maintain
the supporting schedules of the PPE  accounts  to  support  their  reflected
balances as at year-end.

We recommended  that  Management  direct  the  Municipal  Accounting  Office
personnel to provide adjustments in the  books  of  accounts  regarding  the
improperly recorded cash advance transactions for  official  travel  and  to
refer to the Chart of Accounts provided in Volume III of the  GAM  for  LGUs
for reference and guidance in the recording of  the  financial  transactions
of the LGU.

We recommended that Management:


     1. Direct the  Municipal  Accountant  to  record  in  the  appropriate
        Inventory accounts all purchases of  materials  and  supplies  upon
        acceptance of the delivered items and only  recognize  the  expense
        upon consumption or distribution;


     2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer (in  the  absence  of  a  designated
        General  Services  Officer)  to  ensure  that  all  issuances   are
        supported with Requisition and Issue Slips  (RIS)  which  shall  be
        summarized in the Summary of Supplies and Materials  Issued  (SSMI)
        and shall then be submitted to the  Municipal  Accountant  for  the
        recording of the corresponding expense; and


     3. Observe proper  recording  of  transactions  by  referring  to  the
        Revised Chart of Accounts prescribed under COA Circular  No.  2015-
        009 to provide a fair financial statement presentation.

We recommended that Management submit immediately the lacking documents  for
us to make a decision in audit, effect the necessary correcting  entries  on
the  allowance  provided  to  the  employees,  and  strictly   observe   the
guidelines on the grant of benefits, especially on  the  required  documents
to be complied  with  to  preclude  the  issuance  of  audit  suspension  or
disallowance.

We recommended that Management:


     1. Direct Municipal Accountant to look into the details of the unspent
        balances pertaining to fund transfers taken up as liability in  the
        General Fund and Trust Fund books and facilitate the return to  the
        source agency or remittance to the National Treasury or transfer to
        the General Fund of the Agency, whichever is appropriate, and  make
        to the necessary adjustments in the books immediately;


     2. Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Office to  coordinate
        with and assist the Municipal Accountant in verifying  whether  the
        projects/programs associated with the funds received  were  already
        implemented and to secure the necessary documents to facilitate the
        recording of adjustments in the books; and


     3. Ensure proper coordination between the Municipal Accounting  Office
        and the Municipal Planning and Development  Office  in  the  future
        project/program implementation toward funds which may  be  received
        from NGAs.

We recommended that Management:


     1. Explain in writing the reason for  the  payment  of  the  purchased
        heavy equipment despite incomplete delivery;


     2.  Ensure  that  the  payment  for  either  goods  and  services   of
        suppliers/contractors should only be made after the goods have been
        delivered/supplied and/or services have been rendered; and

     3. Submit the lacking necessary documents in order for the Audit  Team
        to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that Management:


     1.  Ensure  immediate  action  and  prompt  conduct   of   procurement
        activities for the timely implementation of the projects; and


     2. Ensure the full utilization of the  appropriated  amounts  for  the
        benefit of the constituents in PPAs to  be  implemented  under  20%
        LGDF.

We recommended that the MDC:


      1. Prepare the required Revised AIP if there are changes in  the  PPAs
         of the original AIP; and


     2. Properly document in its Minutes of Meetings, the presentations and
        deliberations on the changes in  the  PPAs  for  inclusion  in  the
        Revised AIP/Supplemental Investment Programs.

We recommended that Management:


     1. Stop the charging of expenses not allowed to be charged against the
        20% Development Fund;


     2. Adequately plan the projects that will be implemented by  referring
        to the guidelines on 20% Development Fund before proposing it to be
        included as one of the projects to be funded; and


     3. Strictly adhere to the pertinent  provisions  of  law,  rules,  and
        regulations relative to the  utilization  of  the  20%  Development
        Fund, so that constituents could directly enjoy  the  benefits  for
        which the fund was primarily established.

We recommended that Management:


     1. Submit immediately the lacking relief distribution  sheets  or  any
        form of acknowledgment evidencing  that  the  assistance  was  duly
        received by  the  intended  recipients,  as  well  as  the  lacking
        official receipts/cash invoices in order for the Audit Team to make
        a decision in audit; and

     2. Ensure that subsequent transactions pertaining to the  distribution
        of  any  form  of  assistance  must  be  supported  with   complete
        documentation.

We recommended that Management:


      1.  Enforce  the  immediate  liquidation  and/or  settlement  of   all
         outstanding cash advances;


      2.  Stop  the  practice  of  granting  additional  cash  advances   to
         officials/employees who have previous unliquidated cash advances as
         well as granting multiple  cash  advances  to  the  same  employees
         regardless of the purpose;


      3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to expedite  the  request  for  the
         write-off of long outstanding cash  advances  that  are  considered
         dormant in accordance with the guidelines and  procedures  provided
         in COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and

      4. Strictly adhere to the guidelines set forth in COA Circular No. 97-

Recommendations:

      In view of the above observations, we recommended the following:

   1. Create an inventory committee to conduct physical  count  of  PPE.  To

      to instruct their members to cooperate  in  the  conduct  of  physical

      to Other Assets; and

observations.recommendation: We require the submission of the reported missing vouchers in order to preclude the issuance of a Notice of Suspension on the transactions for which the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant could be held jointly and severally liable as prescribed in Section 104 of PD 1445. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer shall ensure the submission of disbursement vouchers and other transaction documents within ten (10) days of the ensuing month as prescribed in COA Circular 2009-006. Moreover, we have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant shall observe the procedures prescribed in Section 44 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) for LGUs, in the recording of check disbursements and submission of vouchers for audit.
observations.reason: Some of the supporting documents that should have been submitted within the prescribed date of submission were not submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation: Fast track the settlement of accounts suspended and to assist the persons liable for the settlement. Adhere to Section 7.1 and 7.2 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We have recommended that Management should strictly monitor the use of government motor vehicles which should be limited to official purposes only through the proper accomplishment of a Driver’s Trip Tickets on a daily basis. Likewise, compliance of a Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption on official travels made is enjoined.

It is recommended that Division Heads and the Municipal Accountant should coordinate in the strict enforcement of the provisions of the General Appropriations Act For FY 2013 (RA 10352), particularly on personnel matters and privileges, and basically on authorized and allowable statutory deductions, so that preparation of payrolls for salaries and wages will be in consonance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, thus, the regularity and propriety of the transaction will not be questioned. Likewise, employees’ eagerness to work will be maintained and that risks on their accountabilities will be prevented and filing of sanctions can be avoided.

We have recommended that the accounting and property section should exert extra effort in observing procedures in the accounting and recording of PPEs as required in Section 41 and 56 of the NGAS Manual for a reliable and informative financial statement. The following steps have to be undertaken: Accounting Section: Maintain PPELC for each ledger accounts under the PPE. Start from the current year and backwards. Determine cut-off period on transaction where data is no longer available for possible requests for authority to write-off as prescribed in COA Circular No. 97-001. Monitor submission of the Physical Count of PPEs and periodically conduct reconciliation for immediate correction in the books of accounts. Recording unserviceable properties reported in the IIRUP in Other Assets and effect adjustment in the PPE. Property Section: Prepare timetable for the conduct of Physical Inventory and coordinate with the Inventory Committee members for a cohesive planning. Update recording of Property Cards to facilitate preparation of the Inventory report to be presented per category of PPEs. Submit the Inventory report required in Section 56 of the NGAS Manual.
observations.reason: There are still some administration expenses not chargeable against the fund and items that caused the unreconciled difference remained unaddressed.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management should implement policies and control procedures in the granting and liquidation of cash advances through the following: Stop the granting of cash advance to official or employee unless the previous cash advance given to him/her is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made in compliance with the above-referred provisions of PD1445; Regularly send confirmation/demand letters as basis in the reconciliation of accounting and accountable officers record and as basis in imposing disciplinary measures; Strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-004 for long outstanding accounts.
observations.reason: Inadequate monitoring of cash advance and the long outstanding cash advances remained unliquidated.

observations.recommendation: Refrain from using privately owned vehicles in official travels in compliance to Rule No. 7 of the Manual on Audit for Fuel Consumption. Also, strengthen controls in the withdrawal and consumption of fuel to ensure expenditures pertaining thereto are not excessive and unnecessary. Properly account for fuel consumption by ensuring that trip tickets and fuel consumption report are properly accomplished and approved.
observations.managementAction: The Management are now will scrutinize thoroughly the vehicles during their Official trips.  We have put up a screening mechanism to arrest and discouraged any employees and officials to use private vehicles in the performance of their respective official duties. But in the event the use of private vehicles is unavoidable the management see to it that the required justifications and supporting documents are readily available. The department that the management will fully concentrate on is the Sangguniang Bayan Department wherein rampant use of private vehicles and subsequently reimbursement for that purpose were being monitored as most often and frequent. The management also mentioned that they will look into the needed control measures.

observations.recommendation: 1. Closely monitor the cash advances granted to officers and employees by issuing Demand Letters, copy furnished this Audit Team for coordinated effort to implement the provision of PD1445 and provisions of COA Circulars on cash advances. 2. The LCE and Accountant should initiate documentation of long overdue accounts of personnel who are no longer in service as a sound practice of fund management. 3. Stop granting cash advances to officials and employees unless the previous cash advance given is first settled or a proper accounting thereof is made. 4. A certification by the Municipal Accountant to the fact as to existence of unliquidated or settled prior cash advance could serve as an attachment to the subsequent claim can be considered a suggested tool or remedy to document processing of DVs on CA.

observations.recommendation: 1. Submit written explanation why the MOA was executed only on Dec. 1, 2009 when the SB Resolution authorizing to enter into contract with Mr. Eddie Go, owner and proprietor of EVG Fishing Industry was approved on Oct. 8, 2007. Include why records/reports showed that the contracting party was able to operate already and reported income/share from business operations since 2008 even without the approved MOA. 2. Concerned Officers/s should also submit related documents/reports on the procedures taken by this LGU in choosing Mr. Go as the Second party to the contract, in compliance with RA 9184. 3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to furnish the Audit Team copies of Annual Financial Reports (AFR) of the fishing boat operations even for CY 2008 and 2009, while waiting for the report for CY 2010 coming from EVG Fishing, and the same be transmitted as soon as received by this LGU, for inclusion in the AAR CY 2010.
observations.managementAction: Provided explanation in the reply to the AAR. The fishing boat was a project of the previous administration and was left dilapidated and abandoned in Brgy. Montserrat. The municipality spent millions in its operations but there was no declared income. The current administration sought to rehabilitate the fishing boat but the cost amounted to P1,700,000.00 There being no funds available for this purpose, the agency scouted for an investor to carry out the said rehabilitation. No other investors were interested except for EVG Fishing Industry owned by Mr. Eddie Go. The Accounting Office is already preparing Annual Financial Report of the fishing boat for 2008, 2009 and 2010.
observations.reason: Only the notarized contract had been submitted. The Annual Financial Reports have not been submitted as of this date.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Bids  and  Awards  Committee  expedite  the
   implementation of the corresponding procurement activities and BUB  Focal
   Persons to fast track the preparation of program of  works  in  order  to
   implement the intended projects.
        We also recommended that the LGU make representation to  the  DA-RO
   XI so that the request for amendments on the  number  of  units  and  the
   prices will be favorably acted upon in order to avoid possible penalties.

We recommended that the  Municipal  Accountant  shall  monitor  the
   utilization of the funds received from national government  agencies  and
   immediately cause the return of the  unutilized  balance  to  the  Source
   Agency after the completion of every project.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should comply with the
   documentary requirements provided under Sec. 3 of COA Circular No.  2012-
   001 to ensure the propriety and validity of the transaction and to record
   fund transfers to barangay under the account code 1-03-03-030 – Due  from
   Local Government Units to ensure proper monitoring of fund releases.


        We  further  recommended  to  enforce  liquidation  of   the   fund
   transferred to these barangays to ensure that this was utilized  for  the
   intended projects.

We recommended that management stop  the  practice  of  utilizing  20%
   Development Fund to other purposes  for  which  the  fund  was  created.  We
   further recommended that preparation of the Annual  Investment  Plan  should
   be compliant with the  provisions  enumerated  in  DBM-DILG  Joint  Circular
   Memorandum 2011-1 and ensure that utilization of the same is  in  accordance
   with the plan. With this, social,  economic  and  environmental  development
   would be achieved by the Municipality.

We recommended the following courses of action:


   1. Utilize the 70%  of  the  DRRMF  in  accordance  with  the  authorized
      purposes under DILG Memorandum Circular 2012-073; and
   2. Exercise prudence and diligence in  utilizing  the  calamity  fund  to
      prevent losses to government funds.

We reiterated  our  prior  year  recommendation  to  reinstate  the
   balances of the unexpended balance since 2013  by  preparing  the  proper
   adjusting entries so as to make it available for social services.

We  recommended  that  Local  School  Board  should  implement   the
   programs, projects and activities identified in the SEF budget to  ensure
   that the fund would be used for the priority expenses. Likewise,  enforce
   liquidation of the fund transferred to these district offices  to  ensure
   the propriety of the disbursement.

We recommended that the Head of the Procurement Entity  (HOPE)  and
   the BAC Chairman to consolidate the procurement  of  similar  or  related
   items with the same dates of requisition or in  close  proximity  to  one
   another, into one purchase request and subject the  same  to  competitive
   bidding  pursuant  to  abovementioned  guidelines  to  ensure  that   the
   Municipality will get the most advantageous price.

We recommended that management refrain from  charging  their  prior
   year’s expenditures to current year’s appropriation to  avoid  incurrence
   of deficiency in appropriations.

of accounts suspended and
to assist the persons liable
for the settlement.
Adhere to Section 7.1 and
7.2 of COA Circular No.
2009-006 dated September
15, 2009.

government motor vehicles
which should be limited to
official purposes only
through the proper
accomplishment of a
Driver’s Trip Tickets on a

compliance of a Monthly
Report of Fuel Consumption

Appropriations Act For FY
2013 (RA 10352),
